behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: source collectors -> aggregation/payload builder -> summary input. A unit test of one function can miss wiring/key-mapping omissions across modules. REAL-INFRA is not required: stub clients can return sentinel data and still expose missing-source handoff bugs. Rejected System because behavior text is about "content passed" (data composition), not real HTTP/network semantics. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because this checks pre-generation input completeness, not model output quality or multi-agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real data-collection and payload-composition modules together with three in-process source stubs that each return unique marker fields. Assert the summary-generation input payload contains markers from all three sources with correct section mapping.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is resilience/state-propagation across modules: source adapter error -> report assembler section fallback -> overall report completion. Unit is too low because the failure appears at module boundaries. Real OS/network is not strictly required; an in-process stub raising timeout/connection exceptions can reproduce the failure mode. Rejected System because behavior text only requires graceful degradation on unavailability, not validating real network stack behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because correctness does not depend on model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Use real orchestration/report modules with two normal source stubs and one failing stub (e.g., timeout exception). Assert process does not abort, report is produced, and only the failed section contains `data unavailable`.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on generated narrative quality (factual fidelity to input values), which is model-output dependent and potentially variable across runs. A deterministic stub would always pass and hide real misrepresentation/hallucination failures. Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires "accurately represents" content quality, not just routing/retry/payload formatting around generation. Rejected Workflow because this behavior describes a single generation quality check, not multiple agentic decisions chained in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real summary-generation model on representative fixtures with known key values; score outputs for factual consistency against source values (e.g., required-value extraction checks and mismatch detection) across repeated runs with acceptance thresholds.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is full external-system path: real service calls + real email transport + inbox delivery. In-process fakes can hide SMTP/auth/recipient-format/transport and delivery-path issues. Rejected Integration because behavior text requires "reaches ... inbox correctly," which is an external-system outcome. Rejected Agentic as minimum because this assertion is delivery/integration correctness of the end-to-end pipeline, not model output quality; generation can be deterministic for this check.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled script in a production-like environment against reachable test endpoints and a real test mailbox. Verify one run produces a delivered email in the configured inbox with expected report structure and sections.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real infrastructure boundaries: email service unreachable condition, filesystem persistence, and cross-run retry behavior. Mocks can hide real connection-failure classes and persistence/permission/path issues. Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires local file persistence and retry on the "next scheduled run," which is OS/process/scheduling behavior beyond pure in-process wiring. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model-decision quality is being validated.
behaviors.test_description: Run the script with email endpoint intentionally unreachable (e.g., blocked test SMTP host). Assert fallback report file is written to disk. Trigger next scheduled execution with email restored; assert persisted report is retried and successfully delivered, then fallback state is cleared/updated correctly.
